title: Plugin Host Dry-Run And Safety Skeleton
steps: Create revdeck-plugin-host crate depending on revdeck-plugin-sdk and revdeck-db.
Implement host APIs for validate_manifest, inspect_manifest, dry_run_object_batch, and test_plugin_directory.
Implement default-deny permission evaluation with structured diagnostics.
Add a process runner skeleton interface, but keep actual execution minimal and safe for M1: no network, no process spawn unless explicitly allowed, output caps and sanitized diagnostics.
Record plugin run audit when host test/dry-run executes against a project, if project context is provided.
convergence_criteria: Permission denial is reported as structured sanitized diagnostics.
Runner skeleton is testable without launching untrusted code by default.
Host crate compiles and has focused tests.

result.summary: Host APIs validate, inspect, dry-run, test plugin directories, and digest audit inputs under a safe M1 skeleton.
result.tests_passed: cargo test -p revdeck-plugin-host passed; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings passed
